steps.caption_md: Comparing a = "ABC" and b = "AC". Row 0 and column 0 are the empty-prefix base case, seeded to 0.
At i=1, j=1 we compare a[0]='A' with b[0]='A'. What happens to dp[1][1]?

predict.prompt_md: a[0] and b[0] are both 'A'. What value goes in dp[1][1]?

choices.label_md: They don't count as a match here, so dp[1][1] = max(dp[0][1], dp[1][0]) = 0

predict.explanation_md: 'A' == 'A', so this element belongs in the common subsequence: dp[1][1] = dp[0][0] + 1 = 1.

steps.caption_md: dp[1][1] = 1 and the fill continues row by row: 'B' vs 'A'/'C' never matches, so those cells just carry the best of their neighbors forward.
At i=3, j=2 we compare a[2]='C' with b[1]='C' — a match: dp[3][2] = dp[2][1] + 1 = 2. The LCS of "ABC" and "AC" has length 2 ("AC").
